title: Coffee and walnut cake
subtitle: Coffee sponge with walnut pieces and coffee buttercream
excerpt: Coffee and walnut cake layers a coffee-flavoured sponge with walnut pieces through the batter against a coffee buttercream filling and topping. The coffee flavour comes from instant coffee dissolved in hot water, which blends into the batter without any bitterness.
sourceNotes: Coffee and walnut cake became a fixture of British café culture and domestic baking in the mid-twentieth century, appearing in women's magazine recipe collections and domestic cookbooks from the 1950s onward. The combination of coffee sponge, walnut pieces, and coffee buttercream is the standard British form.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the sponge layers uniced. Defrost at room temperature and assemble before serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the sponge layers a day ahead and store airtight. The assembled cake keeps for 2 days at room temperature.

glossaryTerms.definition: A concentrated coffee flavouring traditionally used in British baking, made from chicory and coffee extract. Camp coffee (a chicory-coffee syrup) is the classic British brand. For a cleaner coffee flavour, strong instant coffee dissolved in a small amount of hot water works equally well in cakes and buttercreams.

content.text: The coffee in this cake is added as a concentrated solution — instant coffee dissolved in just enough hot water to dissolve it, then cooled before going into the batter and the buttercream. Traditional versions use
, which works too, but strong instant coffee gives a cleaner result. The walnuts go into the batter rather than just on top so every slice has them throughout.

attrs.prepNote: dissolved in 2 tbsp hot water, cooled
dissolved in 1 tbsp hot water, cooled

content.text: Heat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan). Grease two 20 cm round tins and line the bases. Beat {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} until pale and fluffy, about 4 minutes. Beat in the {{eggs}} one at a time, then stir in the {{coffee-instant}} solution. Fold in {{self-raising-flour}} and {{baking-powder}}, then fold through the {{walnuts}}. Divide between the tins and bake for 20–22 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in tins for 5 minutes, then turn onto a rack.

Beat the buttercream {{unsalted-butter}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and {{coffee-instant}} solution until smooth and light. Spread half on the bottom layer, then sandwich with the second sponge. Spread the remaining buttercream over the top. Press walnut halves around the edge to decorate.
